feedingProfile.preferredNpk: balanced liquid houseplant fertilizer (e.g. 20-20-20) at quarter strength every 6-8 weeks in spring and summer (Clemson HGIC); RHS suggests a general balanced liquid feed every few weeks April-October; low fertility needs — overfeeding burns leaf tips and roots

commonIssues: Root rot from soggy soil or sitting in a saucer of standing water — keep moist but never waterlogged and use a pot with drainage
Dramatic drooping/wilting when the compost dries out fully — recovers within hours of a good soak, but repeated wilting yellows leaves
Brown leaf tips from over-fertilizing or excessive drying; brown leaf edges from very dry air — mist or stand on a tray of damp gravel, away from radiators and cold draughts
Failure to flower when light is too low or the plant is under about a year old — survives in shade but rarely blooms there
Direct summer sun scorches the foliage — bright indirect light only
Pests: mealybugs, scale, spider mites, fungus gnats (generally pest-light)
Contains calcium oxalate crystals — toxic to pets and children if chewed (mouth/throat burning); wear gloves when handling
Cold damage below about 4 C (40 F); growth slows below 12 C (55 F) — indoor/frost-free growing only
Prefers being slightly pot-bound; overpotting keeps the mix too wet — repot only 1-2 inches (2.5-5 cm) larger

source.citation: Clemson University HGIC — Peace Lily factsheet: tolerates low light, grows best in bright indirect light, no direct sun; keep soil moist but not soggy, dry slightly between waterings, never leave in standing water (root rot); balanced 20-20-20 liquid fertilizer at quarter strength every 6-8 weeks in spring/summer, overfertilizing burns tips; prefers 68-85 F days, ~10 F cooler nights, avoid below 60 F; likes being slightly pot-bound, repot only 1-2 in larger; pests: fungus gnats, mealybugs, spider mites, scale; calcium oxalate toxicity to pets and children; most varieties 1-4 ft
Clemson University HGIC — Peace Lily factsheet: warm daytime 68-85 F (20-29 C), nights ~10 F cooler, avoid temperatures below 60 F to prevent cold damage; keep away from heat/AC vents

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder — Spathiphyllum (group), kempercode b568: zones 11-12; part shade to full shade, bright filtered light preferred, no direct sun; medium moisture, let soil dry between waterings but do not allow to wilt; 55-40 F slows growth and prolonged exposure below 40 F damages the plant; low fertility needs, fertilize at one-quarter strength; keep somewhat potbound; mealybugs and scale occasionally; 1-6 ft tall

RHS — How to grow peace lilies: water April-October once the top few centimetres of compost are dry, reduce in winter; feed with a general liquid fertiliser every few weeks during the growing season; bright indirect light near a window, not direct summer sun; minimum 12 C (55 F), ideal 12-24 C (55-75 F); moderate humidity — damp gravel tray, mist leaves; pot only slightly larger (avoid overpotting), peat-free multi-purpose compost with perlite 3:1; new plants can stay in original pot 1-2 years; poisonous arum — gloves, keep from children and pets
RHS — How to grow peace lilies: peat-free multi-purpose compost mixed 3:1 with perlite; repot into a pot only slightly larger; container guidance backs containerProfile pot-size and soil tags

RHS — Spathiphyllum wallisii plant profile: herbaceous perennial houseplant from tropical Central America/Colombia; indirect or filtered light, partial shade under glass (deep shade reduces flowering); water and mist freely in growth, high humidity, balanced liquid fertiliser monthly; frost-free only; poisonous

Confidence note: baseIntervalDays=7 converts 'water once the top few centimetres are dry' (RHS) / 'moist but dries slightly between waterings' (Clemson) into a typical indoor weekly cadence — not stated as a number by any source; hotWeatherMultiplier=0.8 is a conservative planner estimate

Confidence note: lightProfile is expressed as DIRECT sun hours (target 1, max 3) translated from 'bright indirect light, no direct sun' guidance — sources give qualitative light levels, not sun-hours; treat as bright-indirect placement, not outdoor sun exposure

Confidence note: recommendedMinLiters=2 (~15 cm pot) and ideal 3-12 L are planner conversions from 'keep slightly pot-bound, repot 1-2 in larger' (Clemson/MoBot/RHS) and typical 1-4 ft mature size — no source states liters. heatStressC=30 derived from Clemson's 85 F upper preferred bound; coldHardyMinC=4 from MoBot's damage threshold below 40 F. Seasonality months derived from RHS April-October growing season for an indoor plant
